plants: A green film or trailing strand in the water, simple and ancient, thriving wherever light reaches.
Rooted in the mud below but pushing its stems and leaves up into the air, living in two worlds at once.
Drifting on the surface with roots trailing like threads beneath, going wherever the current decides.
Tough and fleshy, growing in salt-crusted soil where the taste of the sea is in everything.
Rooted in black mud with its shoots rising into the air, marking the border between water and land.
Thriving in waterlogged ground that would drown anything else, its roots at home in the muck.
Long ribbons swaying in the current, forming underwater forests where small things hide and drift.
Living entirely beneath the surface, waving gently in the current, only its flowers sometimes breaking into the air.
